full_prompt: Design the base frame of a Mobile Launch Tower for a small-launcher launch-site benchmark, per a student ground-segment design brief. The tower supports a 50 t launch vehicle (fueled) at T-0 and must carry a 1 MN hold-down force per umbilical arm with four arms total (4 MN total hold-down). Tower overall height 30 m, base footprint 8 m x 8 m square. Base frame primary material: AS/NZS 3679.1 Grade 350 structural steel (Young's modulus 200 GPa, Poisson 0.30, yield 350 MPa, ultimate 450 MPa, density 7850 kg/m^3) rolled sections. Four outer columns built from UC 310 x 283 kg/m W-sections; cross-bracing from CHS 200 x 8 mm tubes. Base anchor bolts: 12 x M48 Grade 10.9 into launch-site concrete mat. Load cases per Eurocode EN 1990 + EN 1991-1-4 wind + ISO 19901 offshore-ish launch environment: (LC1) dead load: tower self-weight 200 t + launch vehicle 50 t = 2450 kN vertical on base frame; (LC2) pre-launch hold-down: 4 MN upward at the four umbilical arm tips + 2450 kN down = 1550 kN net upward on base; (LC3) ignition transient: 1 MN horizontal blast load on one side of tower for 500 ms; (LC4) wind 50 m/s ULS (1/100 yr): 1.0 kPa dynamic pressure x 30 m tower x ~8 m projected = 240 kN horizontal at mid-height; (LC5) seismic 0.2 g horizontal per site design spectrum; (LC6) T-0 release: 4 MN hold-down instantaneous release = 4 MN downward rebound on base for 100 ms. Pass/fail per EN 1993-1 (γM0 = 1.00, γM2 = 1.25): (R1) max von Mises in W-sections under LC1-LC5 <= 350 MPa (γM0 yield); (R2) base anchor bolt tension under LC2 + LC3 <= 660 kN per bolt (M48 Grade 10.9 tensile capacity 910 kN / 1.38 ULS combined with shear); (R3) horizontal deflection at tower top under LC4 wind <= H/200 = 150 mm; (R4) base frame mass <= 50 t; (R5) first sway mode above 2 Hz to stay clear of ignition transient excitation; (R6) linear buckling eigenvalue of columns under LC2 upward reaction >= 2.5. Deliverables: STEP AP242, FEA per case, anchor-bolt tension table, modal, buckling eigenvalue, and mass report.
short_prompt:
Design the base frame of a Mobile Launch Tower supporting a 50 t (fueled) small launcher at T-0 with four umbilical-arm hold-downs of 1 MN each (4 MN total). Tower height 30 m, base footprint 8 x 8 m, primary material class: Grade 350 structural steel (AS/NZS 3679.1 / EN 10025 S355 analog) rolled sections (UC 310 x 283 columns, CHS 200 x 8 braces); 12 x M48 Grade 10.9 anchor bolts into the concrete mat. Design per Eurocode EN 1990 + EN 1991-1-4 (wind) + EN 1993-1 steel (gamma_M0 = 1.00, gamma_M2 = 1.25), with ISO 19901 launch-environment context. Target base frame mass <= 50 t.

Load cases.
- LC1 dead: 200 t tower self-weight + 50 t vehicle = 2450 kN vertical.
- LC2 pre-launch hold-down: 4 MN up at the umbilicals + 2450 kN down = 1550 kN net up on the base.
- LC3 ignition blast: 1 MN horizontal on one side for 500 ms.
- LC4 wind 50 m/s ULS (1/100 yr): 1.0 kPa dynamic pressure x 30 m x ~8 m projected = 240 kN at mid-height.
- LC5 seismic: 0.2 g horizontal.
- LC6 T-0 release: 4 MN downward rebound for 100 ms.

prompt.title: Mobile launch tower base frame
prompt.brief: 30 m tower base frame for 50 t launcher, 4 MN hold-down, Eurocode-compliant.

notes.needs_rulebook_fetch_reason: Launch-site ground-segment benchmark has no dedicated public rulebook; numerics anchored to Eurocode EN 1990/1993-1 + ECSS-E-ST-32-10C + typical small-launcher T-0 loads.
